turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what appliance can I help you with today?
Hi, I have a question about my dryer — not really a problem, more general upkeep.
Sure, happy to help with that — what did you want to know?
How often should I actually clean the dryer vent duct to avoid issues?

Good question — per our appliance library's general maintenance guide, clean the exterior vent duct at least once a year, and sooner if drying times have gotten longer or the outside vent flap barely moves while the dryer runs. A partially blocked vent makes the dryer run hotter and work harder than it should, which is exactly the kind of thing that eventually shows up as a heating problem. If the run is long or has several bends, it's worth having a technician clean it periodically instead of guessing. (Source: docs/library/general_maintenance_tips.md)
Anytime! Let me know if you notice any actual performance issues and I can help troubleshoot.
